title: notholaena candida var. lutea
intro: Notholaena candida var. lutea, also known as the golden maidenhair fern, is a type of flowering plant in the family Pteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. The health benefits of Notholaena candida var. lutea include reducing inflammation and relieving symptoms associated with colds and flu. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to promote respiratory health and alleviate congestion.
